generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance ManufacturingContextualDataCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects contextual data traces from the physical manufacturing system including sensor readings, operational states, environmental conditions, and performance metrics. Operates passively without autonomous feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ManufacturingContextualDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ManufacturingContextualDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingContextualDataCollector
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLRequirementsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based model capturing requirements layer of the manufacturing system using MBSE methodologies. Defines system requirements across multiple abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model defining system functions and their relationships using Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) for component-connector perspectives."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based logical architecture model detailing component interfaces, interconnections, and composition hierarchies across the manufacturing system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLProductTechnicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based product/technical layer model providing detailed structural and behavioral descriptions of the manufacturing system components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model for behavioral modeling and state machine definitions (SysML v1.7 compatible) of the manufacturing system dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcAutomationEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed dynamic simulations of the manufacturing system's behavioral aspects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces from the physical manufacturing system including validated, filtered, and normalized sensor readings and operational metrics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingContextualDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) framework enabling multi-layered abstraction modeling (requirements, functional, logical, and product/technical layers) for the manufacturing system."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelIntegrationService
]

instance MontiArcAutomationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Architecture Description Language (ADL) enabling component-connector modeling with interfaces, interconnections, and composition hierarchies for the manufacturing system."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentConnectorIntegrationService
    DTDFVocab:enables StateMachineBehaviorService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink and other simulation tools enabling detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic simulations of the manufacturing system."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance CppJavaImplementationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++/Java programming languages enabling general-purpose implementation of system logic and integration with modeling tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables CodeIntegrationService
]

instance AutomatedDataFlowEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated one-way data flow mechanism for passive collection and abstraction of data from physical manufacturing system to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring consistency and integration of SysML models across different abstraction layers (requirements, functional, logical, product/technical)."
    DTDFVocab:provides ManufacturingSystemModelConsistencyInsight
]

instance ComponentConnectorIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing component interfaces, interconnections, and composition hierarchies using MontiArc ADL."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentConnectorConsistencyInsight
]

instance StateMachineBehaviorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling behavioral modeling and state machine definitions using MontiArc Automaton and SysML v1.7."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralModelConsistencyInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing detailed dynamic simulations of the manufacturing system using Simulink and other simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultInsight
]

instance CodeIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling integration of C++/Java implementations with modeling tools and simulation environments."
    DTDFVocab:provides CodeModelIntegrationInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling automated collection, validation, filtering, and normalization of contextual data traces from the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ManufacturingSystemModelConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing verification of model consistency across SysML's multiple abstraction layers (requirements, functional, logical, product/technical)."
]

instance ComponentConnectorConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight verifying consistency of component interfaces, interconnections, and composition hierarchies defined in MontiArc ADL."
]

instance BehavioralModelConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring consistency between state machine definitions and behavioral models across SysML v1.7 and MontiArc Automaton."
]

instance SimulationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing detailed simulation results from Simulink and other behavioral simulation tools for manufacturing system dynamics."
]

instance CodeModelIntegrationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight verifying successful integration between C++/Java implementations and SysML/Simulink modeling environments."
]

instance DataQualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight assessing quality of aggregated contextual data traces including validation, filtering, and normalization status."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ManufacturingContextualDataCollector,
                   SysMLRequirementsModel,
                   SysMLFunctionalArchitectureModel,
                   SysMLLogicalArchitectureModel,
                   SysMLProductTechnicalModel,
                   MontiArcAutomationModel,
                   SimulinkBehavioralSimulationModel,
                   ManufacturingContextualData,
                   SysMLModelingEnabler,
                   MontiArcAutomationEnabler,
                   SimulationEnabler,
                   CppJavaImplementationEnabler,
                   AutomatedDataFlowEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system leverages real-time data-driven representations, as implied by the abstract and introduction, which require live-data linking between the physical and digital components. The system streams sensor data via **MQTT**, a lightweight publish-subscribe protocol optimized for low-latency, high-frequency data transmission, enabling time-series analysis. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed, the use of MQTT suggests support for near-real-time or real-time synchronization, with temporal granularity dependent on the underlying sensor sampling rates and the system's ability to process continuous data streams. The integration of simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) further implies the need for time-aligned behavioral modeling, though specific time scales (e.g., microseconds, milliseconds) are not specified in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) constellation** in this context is implicitly defined through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and **architecture description languages (ADLs)**. The orchestration and coordination of the DT system are structured around **multi-layered abstraction models** (requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers), which are decomposed using frameworks like **RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**. The **component & connector perspective** of ADLs (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc, SysML IBDs) provides the foundational architecture pattern for integrating heterogeneous components, where **interfaces, interconnections, and composition rules** govern system-wide coordination. Behavioral descriptions (e.g., state machines in SysML v1.7 or MontiArc Automaton) enable dynamic orchestration, while integration with **simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods)** or **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)** ensures real-time or near-real-time synchronization. Resource management is not explicitly detailed, but the **passive-to-active transition** from Digital Shadow to DT (as per Kritzinger et al.) implies the need for **active components** (e.g., data processing, inference, or control modules) to interpret and act upon data flows. The system-wide coordination relies on **heterogeneous toolchains** and **stakeholder integration**, requiring **interoperability mechanisms** (e.g., data exchange formats, APIs) but lacks specific orchestration protocols (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **model fidelity** through structured validation and verification (V&V) processes, though specific methodologies are not detailed in the provided context. Uncertainty quantification and error detection are implied through the integration of **automated data flows** from physical systems (e.g., Digital Shadows), where incoming data is passively collected and requires **active software components** (e.g., DT-specific modules) for interpretation and processing. Quality assurance is inherently tied to **MBSE methodologies**, which enforce traceability across abstraction layers (requirements → functional/logical → technical). However, explicit error correction mechanisms, uncertainty propagation frameworks, or formal validation protocols (e.g., statistical testing, model calibration) are not specified in the document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the **communication flow** between physical and digital components (as described in the Digital Shadow Reference Model [32]) implies the need for a **reliable, low-latency network** to support one-way data transmission from the physical object to its digital shadow. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, HTTP/REST), network topology (e.g., star, mesh), or bandwidth requirements are not mentioned, the system must accommodate **heterogeneous data sources** (e.g., sensors, IoT devices) and integrate with **modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink) for real-time or batch processing. Security protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) are not specified, but the passive nature of the digital shadow suggests minimal active intervention in network communication. Redundancy mechanisms or fault tolerance strategies are also not addressed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems via **MQTT**, a lightweight protocol explicitly mentioned for streaming time-series sensor data, enabling interoperability with IoT devices and enterprise systems. While **OPC UA** is not explicitly named, the use of MQTT suggests adherence to **industrial IoT standards** for data exchange. The system supports heterogeneous toolchains, including **MBSE modeling languages (SysML, ADLs)**, **simulation tools (Simulink)**, and **general-purpose programming languages (C++, Java)**, requiring seamless data and model interoperability. Dependencies on external systems are implied by the **one-way data flow** of the Digital Shadow (Kritzinger et al.), where physical objects push data to the digital representation, but specific integration patterns (e.g., APIs, middleware) or enterprise system dependencies (e.g., ERP, MES) are **Not in Document**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safety cell** for the robotic asset (e.g., MABI robotic arm, referenced in [44]), which physically restricts the maximum working range to mitigate collision risks. Data exchange in the system leverages **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** as a lightweight protocol for streaming real-time operational data ([52]). No explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., OAuth 2.0, JWT, TLS 1.2/1.3) or compliance with standards like IEC 62443 or ISO 26262 are documented. The Asset Administration Shell (AAS) framework ([46], [47], [50]) provides a structured approach to asset lifecycle management but does not detail access control mechanisms or threat mitigation strategies beyond the physical safety constraints of the robotic environment."
]
